results.description: 19-year-old female browsing for herself — expects fashion/cosmetics lean
results.label: baseline
results.agent_response: The Floral Printed Dress at Rs. 2,800 is such a cute pick, Sashika — perfect for a casual day out! The Lip Gloss Set at Rs. 950 is also a steal if you're into a little glam.
failures: completeness: Agent jumped straight to specific products without asking any clarifying question — the user said 'just looking around' and needs guidance, not an immediate pitch
personalization: Used Sashika's name once but didn't leverage age/context beyond the product choices; felt slightly generic
results.what_was_wrong: The agent skipped the expected clarifying question entirely and immediately pushed specific products on a user who explicitly said they were just browsing, making the response feel pushy and premature rather than helpful.
results.what_was_good: The tone is warm and casual, the products suggested (dress and lip gloss) are age-appropriate and gender-relevant, and the name was used naturally.

results.description: 45-year-old male looking for electronics — direct product intent
results.label: baseline
results.agent_response: We've got some great options for you, Ruwan — the Sony Wireless Headphones at Rs. 12,500, the Smart Watch Fitness Tracker at Rs. 8,900, or the Portable Bluetooth Speaker at Rs. 5,400. Any of these catch your eye, or are you looking for something specific?
failures: personalization: Used Ruwan's name but ignored age/gender context — no tailoring to a 45-year-old male's likely needs or preferences

product_quality: Suggested generic, youth-skewing items (fitness tracker, Bluetooth speaker) without considering what a 45-year-old professional male might actually want (e.g., home audio, laptop accessories, smart home devices)
results.what_was_wrong: The product suggestions feel generic and not meaningfully tailored to a 45-year-old male — a fitness tracker and Bluetooth speaker are not strong first recommendations for this demographic without more context.
results.what_was_good: The response is concise, includes prices, uses the customer's name, and ends with a clear question to move the conversation forward.

results.description: Very low budget under 500 LKR — honesty required
results.label: baseline
results.agent_response: The Kandos Chocolate Bar (Rs. 180) and a Greeting Card Birthday (Rs. 250) together come to exactly Rs. 430 — just a tiny bit over, Thilini, but if you can stretch just Rs. 30 more, it's a lovely little combo! Or the chocolate bar alone at Rs. 180 keeps you well within budget.
failures: personalization: Used her name but didn't leverage her age (24, female) to tailor suggestions more specifically — a young woman might appreciate beauty/skincare samples or trendy snacks beyond just chocolate
completeness: Suggested only two products without asking what the occasion or need is, limiting the helpfulness; also didn't mention what other options exist within Rs. 400 strictly

product_quality: The combo suggestion slightly exceeds the stated Rs. 400 budget, which contradicts the honesty requirement — even framing it as 'just Rs. 30 more' is a mild upsell nudge on a very tight budget
results.what_was_wrong: The suggested combo (Rs. 430) exceeds the user's stated Rs. 400 budget and nudging her to spend more, however gently, is not ideal when honesty and respecting a strict low budget are the core requirements.
results.what_was_good: The response was warm, used her name, gave a concrete within-budget fallback option, and didn't make her feel embarrassed about her budget.

results.description: Vague request — just get me something nice
results.label: baseline
results.agent_response: What do you usually enjoy most, Chathura — tech gadgets, grooming, or something for the home?
failures: relevance: The response skips the most important clarifying question — who is this for and what occasion — and jumps to product categories instead
personalization: Uses the name but ignores the core expected quality: asking about recipient and occasion, which is the primary missing context
product_quality: Offering category guesses (tech, grooming, home) without knowing the occasion or recipient is premature and potentially misguided
completeness: Asking about product category before knowing who the gift is for and why misses the logical first step, leaving the conversation less useful
results.what_was_wrong: The agent skipped the single most important clarifying question — who the gift is for and what occasion — and instead jumped prematurely to product category preferences, which violates the core expected quality for this scenario.
results.what_was_good: The tone was warm and conversational, and the message was concise and non-robotic.

results.description: Gifting: 25-year-old male sending to 55-year-old mother in Colombo
results.label: baseline
results.agent_response: What a lovely thing to do, Dinesh! Does your mum enjoy pampering treats, sweets, or something classic and beautiful?
failures: product_quality: Suggestions are vague and generic — 'pampering treats, sweets, or something classic and beautiful' doesn't reflect age-appropriate specifics for a 55-year-old Sri Lankan mother
completeness: Did not confirm Colombo delivery is possible, and did not ask about budget or occasion — missing key qualifying questions to move the conversation forward effectively
results.what_was_good: Warm, natural tone that uses Dinesh's name and immediately frames it as a gifting scenario, with a follow-up question that attempts to narrow preferences.

results.description: Birthday with tight deadline — tomorrow delivery
results.label: baseline
results.agent_response: The Indoor Succulent Set (Rs. 1,800) or the Garden Tool Kit Gift Set (Rs. 2,900) would make a wonderful birthday gift for your mum, Amali — both are perfect for someone who loves planting! Would you like to go with the more budget-friendly Succulent Set, or treat her to the full Garden Tool Kit?
failures: completeness: The agent failed to acknowledge or address the urgent tomorrow delivery deadline — a critical factor the user raised
results.what_was_good: The agent correctly interpreted 'planting' as gardening, suggested genuinely relevant products with prices, used the user's name warmly, and posed a clear follow-up question to move the conversation forward.

results.description: Parents anniversary — sender unsure what to get
results.label: baseline
results.agent_response: The Chocolate + Wine Gift Hamper at LKR 6,800 would be such a thoughtful anniversary gift for your parents, Kasun — it feels celebratory and special without being overdone. The Mixed Flower Bouquet Large at LKR 4,200 is also a classic that never fails, or you could pair it with the Couple Photo Frame Silver (LKR 1,500) for something more personal and lasting. Want to go with one of these, or would combining a couple of them make it feel extra special?
failures: completeness: The agent jumped straight to product recommendations without asking a single clarifying question (budget, parents' tastes, location) — violating a key expected quality
personalization: Used Kasun's name but ignored his age (23, likely a young adult with budget constraints) and made no acknowledgment of his uncertainty or the emotional significance of the occasion
relevance: Skipped the discovery phase entirely — user explicitly said 'I have no idea what to get', signalling a need for guidance before product pitches
results.what_was_wrong: The agent skipped asking any clarifying question and jumped directly to product suggestions, ignoring the user's stated uncertainty and missing the chance to personalise recommendations around budget or preferences.
results.what_was_good: The suggested products (hamper, flowers, photo frame) are genuinely couple-appropriate and the tone is warm and friendly rather than robotic.

results.description: Gift from a child to a parent — age gap dynamic
results.label: baseline
results.agent_response: The Coffee Mug with a Dad Quote (Rs. 750) is such a sweet and personal pick, or if you want to go a little bigger, the Men's Grooming Kit (Rs. 1,800) or the Genuine Leather Wallet (Rs. 2,200) would make your dad feel really special, Nethu! Which one feels right to you?
failures: personalization: Used name well but didn't acknowledge the child-gifting-parent dynamic or Nethu's likely limited budget as a 14-year-old — led with the cheapest but framed all three as roughly equal options
product_quality: Grooming kit and wallet are solid but no brief rationale for why they suit a 48-year-old dad specifically; coffee mug with a quote is good but could feel generic without more context
completeness: Didn't ask about budget range or offer to narrow down further based on what Nethu can afford
results.what_was_good: The tone is warm, encouraging, and perfectly age-appropriate for a 14-year-old buying a Father's Day gift — not condescending, and the price ladder from Rs. 750 upward shows budget awareness.

results.description: Delivery deadline — needs to arrive by Sunday
results.label: baseline
results.agent_response: Great question, Pradeep! Kapruka delivers island-wide, and Sunday delivery is possible depending on your location — orders placed today typically make it in time. What are you looking to send or order?
failures: product_quality: No products were suggested or asked about — this dimension is effectively N/A but the agent missed a chance to ask what they want to buy
personalization: Used the name but didn't leverage any other profile context (age/gender) and the greeting feels slightly formulaic
completeness: Did not ask for delivery location/city, which is critical to confirming Sunday feasibility — this is a key missing step
relevance: Said 'orders placed today typically make it in time' without knowing the city — this is a soft overpromise without the necessary caveat
results.what_was_wrong: The agent failed to ask for the delivery city/area, which is essential before confirming Sunday delivery feasibility, and slightly overpromised by saying orders 'typically make it in time' without that information.
results.what_was_good: The tone is warm and natural, the response is concise, and it correctly pivots to asking what the user wants to order.

results.description: Same-day delivery to Kandy
results.label: baseline
results.agent_response: Great news, Shanika — yes, Kapruka does offer same-day delivery to Kandy! What are you looking to send?
failures: relevance: Confirmed same-day delivery without caveats — Kapruka's same-day delivery to Kandy is not guaranteed for all items and the agent stated it as fact without qualification

product_quality: No product categories mentioned at all — agent missed the opportunity to indicate which types of items (e.g. cakes, flowers, groceries) typically qualify for same-day delivery to Kandy
personalization: Used Shanika's name but made no use of any other profile context; superficial personalization

completeness: Asked a follow-up question which is good, but failed to proactively share useful information (e.g. category hints, cutoff times) that would help move the conversation forward meaningfully
results.what_was_wrong: The agent confidently confirmed same-day delivery to Kandy without any caveats or category specifics, risking a false promise and missing the chance to guide Shanika toward qualifying product types.
results.what_was_good: The tone was warm and natural, and asking what she wants to send was a sensible next step.

results.description: Caller in London, recipient in Colombo, Vesak next week
results.label: baseline
results.agent_response: The Handmade Vesak Lantern (LKR 1,200) or the Traditional Oil Lamp Set (LKR 2,800) would be such a meaningful surprise for your parents, Nathasha — both feel beautifully festive for the occasion. Would you like to add the Assorted Sweets Vesak Gift Box (LKR 1,900) to make it an extra special delivery?
failures: completeness: Response jumps straight to product recommendations without acknowledging the international sender context, confirming Kapruka can deliver to Colombo from London, or asking about budget/preferences — critical steps before pitching products
personalization: Uses Nathasha's name but ignores the key context that she is overseas sending to parents, which is the defining emotional and logistical element of this scenario
relevance: Does not address the international gifting capability or reassure her that delivery from London to Colombo is possible and reliable
results.what_was_wrong: The response skips the essential reassurance about international delivery and jumps directly to product pitches without first confirming Kapruka can handle the London-to-Colombo send, or asking about her budget — making it feel presumptuous and incomplete.
results.what_was_good: Product suggestions (lantern, oil lamp set, sweets box) are genuinely culturally appropriate for Vesak and feel warm and festive, with a natural, human tone.

results.description: Multi-item cart: cake + flowers together
results.label: baseline
results.agent_response: Absolutely, Ishara! You can pair the Chocolate Birthday Cake 1kg (Rs. 3,200) with the Red Rose Bouquet 12 Stems (Rs. 2,800) or the White Lily Bouquet (Rs. 2,200) in one order — a gorgeous combo for a birthday. Which flowers do you prefer?
failures: personalization: Used name well but didn't leverage age/gender context to tailor cake or flower suggestions further
completeness: Confirmed multi-item order is possible but didn't explicitly mention they'd be delivered together, which was an expected quality
results.what_was_good: The response immediately confirmed multi-item ordering, offered specific products with prices, and ended with a clear question to move the conversation forward — all in a warm, natural tone.

results.description: Multi-turn cart: add, add another, remove one
results.label: baseline
results.agent_response: Got it, Tharaka! Cake removed — just the flowers in your cart now. Ready to checkout when you are!
results.what_was_good: Concise, smooth, and natural — acknowledged the removal instantly, confirmed the updated cart state, used the name, and moved forward without any friction or pushback.

results.description: Tanglish input: machan I need something for my amma
results.label: baseline
results.agent_response: Ayyo, last minute but we got you machan! The Chocolate Box Premium at LKR 1,800 is a safe bet, or if you want to go a bit more special, the Flower Bouquet Mixed at LKR 2,500 would make her day — what do you think she'd love more, Kavinda?
failures: language_match: Uses 'machan' and 'Ayyo' well but could lean a bit more into Tanglish phrasing throughout — feels slightly more English than Tanglish in the middle
personalization: Uses the name Kavinda well but doesn't leverage age/gender context to tailor product angle (e.g. young guy buying for mom angle)
product_quality: Both suggestions are solid but no mention of delivery urgency for tomorrow — doesn't confirm if next-day delivery is actually available
results.what_was_good: The response nails the casual, warm Sri Lankan tone with 'Ayyo machan' opener, acknowledges the urgency naturally, offers two concrete options at different price points, and ends with a personalised question to keep the conversation moving.

results.description: Sinhala script input — full Sinhala response required
results.label: baseline
results.agent_response: Sumudu, ඔබේ දියණියට වයස කීයද — ඒ අනුව හොඳම තෑග්ග suggest කරන්නම්! 😊
failures: product_quality: No products were suggested at all — the agent only asked a clarifying question without offering any preliminary ideas
completeness: While asking the daughter's age is smart, the agent could have offered a few gift categories or examples to move the conversation forward while waiting for the answer
language_match: 'suggest' is an English word used mid-sentence — a more natural Sinhala phrasing like 'යෝජනා කරන්නම්' would be more appropriate
results.what_was_wrong: The response asks for clarification but offers no product suggestions or gift categories at all, making it feel like a dead end rather than a helpful starting point.
results.what_was_good: The tone is warm, natural, and genuinely conversational Sinhala — asking the daughter's age is a smart and relevant clarifying question.

results.description: Mid-conversation language switch English to Tanglish
results.label: baseline
results.agent_response: Malith machang, the Ferrero Rocher Box 24 pcs (Rs. 3,200) is a solid pick, and if you want to go the extra mile, pair it with the Enchanted Forest Perfume 50ml (Rs. 4,800) — she'll love it! Want to add both to cart or go with just one?
failures: personalization: Used the name well but didn't leverage the 26-year-old male gifting context deeply — could have nudged toward a romantic combo angle more explicitly
product_quality: Only one chocolate and one perfume shown — a slight variety (two options per category) would help the user choose better
results.what_was_good: Nailed the Tanglish register instantly with 'machang', kept the tone warm and casual, addressed both requested categories, and moved the conversation forward with a clear cart CTA.

results.description: User changes mind after adding to cart
results.label: baseline
results.agent_response: No problem, Dilhara! The red roses have been removed. For lilies, the White Lily Bouquet (Rs. 2,600) is a classic, or if you'd like something a bit more lush, the Mixed Lily Vase (Rs. 3,800) is stunning — which one catches your eye?
results.what_was_good: Handled the change of mind gracefully with no friction, immediately pivoted to lily options with concrete product suggestions and prices, keeping the conversation moving naturally forward.

results.description: Product category Kapruka likely doesn't carry — custom furniture
results.label: baseline
results.agent_response: We don't carry custom-built wooden furniture, Renuka, but the Storage Ottoman Coffee Table at LKR 8,900 is a gorgeous, functional piece that could anchor your living room beautifully. Would a coffee table work for what you have in mind, or are you looking for something specific like shelving or seating?
results.what_was_good: Honest, direct pivot with a specific product and price point, followed by an engaging follow-up question that keeps the conversation moving naturally.

results.description: User gives conflicting information — says gift for friend, then for self
results.label: baseline
results.agent_response: The Casio G-Shock (Rs. 15,000) is a bold, durable pick, Gayan — or if you prefer something more classic, the Stainless Analog looks sharp at Rs. 8,500. Which style speaks to you more?
failures: product_quality: Only two watches mentioned with minimal detail — no images, links, or broader range shown for a 27-year-old male
completeness: Could have offered a third option or price range variety to better move toward a purchase decision
results.what_was_good: Smoothly pivoted from gifting context without any awkwardness, used the name naturally, and offered a clear style contrast between two watches to engage Gayan's preference.

results.description: Specific request with no budget mentioned — perfume
results.label: baseline
results.agent_response: The Engage Floral Perfume at LKR 1,900 is a great everyday pick, Nadeesha, and if you're looking for something a little more luxurious, the Body Shop White Musk at LKR 3,800 has a beautiful soft scent. Do you prefer something light and fresh or a bit more bold and intense?
failures: completeness: Only two products shown — expected guidelines say show a range of price points from affordable to premium, this response is too thin

product_quality: Only two options presented with minimal description; no mention of scent profiles suited to a 25-year-old female (floral, fresh, feminine); White Musk at 3,800 is not 'premium' — no high-end option shown
personalization: Name used once but age/gender context not leveraged meaningfully — no mention of scent types suited to her demographic or lifestyle
results.what_was_wrong: The response shows only two products with sparse descriptions and no premium option, falling short of the expected range and richness of recommendations for a young female buyer.
results.what_was_good: The tone is warm and conversational, and the closing question to gauge preference (light/fresh vs bold) is a smart way to refine recommendations.
